approval-flow.fallback-rules-hint: Fallback rules are evaluated only when no other rules match.

node.description: Only one approval is required on this node when there are multiple approvers.

preset-descriptions.drop-or-truncate: Require approval for DROP TABLE or TRUNCATE statements.
preset-descriptions.fallback: A catch-all rule that matches all requests. Place at the end of your rules list.
preset-descriptions.high-affected-rows: Require approval when affected rows exceed 100.

issue-review.and-n-other-users: {names} and 1 other user | {names} and {count} other users

disallow-approve-reason.some-task-checks-are-still-running: Some task checks are still running.

issue-review.self-approval-not-allowed: Self-approval is not allowed in this project. So you cannot approve your own issue.

risk.description: Risk assessment automatically evaluates database changes and assigns risk levels based on the type of operation being performed.
risk.how-it-works-description: When you create a database change, Bytebase automatically analyzes the SQL statements and assigns a risk level (Low, Moderate, or High) based on predefined rules.
risk.integration-description: Risk levels integrate with the Custom Approval system. You can configure approval flows that require different levels of review based on the assessed risk level of a change.
risk.risk-level-high: High risk operations include destructive changes like DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, or large-scale data modifications.
risk.risk-level-low: Low risk operations include SELECT queries and minor schema changes that are unlikely to cause issues.
risk.risk-level-moderate: Moderate risk operations include standard DDL changes like adding columns or indexes.

rule.first-match-wins: Rules are evaluated in order. First matching rule applies.

extra-params.description: Additional parameters for database connection string

data-source.private-key-passphrase-placeholder: Leave empty if your private key is not encrypted
data-source.private-key-passphrase-tip: Optional passphrase for encrypted private keys

ssl.verify-certificate-tooltip: Enable TLS certificate verification for secure connections. Disable only for development or when certificates cannot be properly validated (e.g., self-signed certs, VPN environments). Warning: Disabling verification is insecure

database.access-denied: You don't have the permission to access this database.

classification.description: Define classification levels to apply consistent masking policies across columns and databases.

database.engine-not-supported-for-backup: Prior backup is not supported for: {databases}
database.incorrect-project-warning: SQL editor can only query databases in projects available to the user. You should first transfer this database into a project.
database.mixed-label-values-warning: Some of the selected resources have mixed values for the same key. Only edit these key-value pairs if you intend to change them.
database.mixed-values-for-label: This selected label have mixed values
database.no-environment: This database has not been bound to the environment

revision.drag-drop-or-click: Drag and drop files here, or click to select
revision.files-already-imported-description: These files' versions already exist in the target database and cannot be imported again

revision.invalid-version-format: Version must be numeric parts separated by dots (e.g., 1.0.0)

revision.no-releases-found: No releases found in this project

revision.select-release-description: Select a release to import revisions from
revision.upload-files-description: Upload SQL files from your local computer to create database revisions

sync-schema.description: Bytebase supports synchronizing a schema to one or multiple target databases. (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, MSSQL, TiDB only)

message.select-a-target-database-first: Please select a target database first.

sync-schema.synchronize-statements-description: You can further edit the generated DDL statements

table.select-tip: Please select a table to start.

catalog.description: Edit and upload the table catalog.

db.failed-to-sync-schema-for-database-datab: Failed to sync schema for database '{0}'.

environment.delete-description: Delete environment will also remove it from related instances and databases. You cannot undo this action

export-data.password-info: Provide a password to encrypt the export file.

checklist.database-group-recommendation: For databases sharing the same schema, a database group lets you migrate them together in one change. Define the group once and reuse it across migrations without picking individual databases every time.
checklist.title: Checks before we start
checklist.wif-notice-suffix: to set up GitOps using a service account.
checklist.wif-notice-text: Workload Identity Federation is currently supported for GitHub and GitLab only. For other VCS, refer to the tutorials for

overview.description: Bytebase GitOps lets you manage Database as Code by integrating database schema changes into your CI/CD pipeline.
overview.description-git: Your database schema lives in Git alongside your application code. Pull requests trigger SQL review, and merging to the target branch automatically creates rollouts.
overview.title: What is GitOps

test-setup.description: Verify the workflow is working end-to-end with a sample migration file.
test-setup.naming-convention: Migration files are applied in lexicographic order. Use a timestamp or sequence prefix, e.g. 20240101000000_create_table.sql. Append _ddl or _dml to indicate the change type.
test-setup.step-create-branch: Create a new branch, add the file above, and open a pull request targeting {branch}.
test-setup.step-merge: Once the review passes, merge the PR. Bytebase will automatically create a rollout and deploy to the target environments.
test-setup.step-sql-review: SQL Review runs automatically on the pull request and posts review comments.
test-setup.title: Test your first GitOps migration

workflow.description: Copy the workflow files into your repository to automate SQL review and database rollouts.
workflow.provider-not-match: The selected workload identity only works for {provider}.
workflow.title: Workflow file generation

identity-provider.claims-description: The following claims were returned by the identity provider. This information can help you configure field mappings.
identity-provider.delete-warning: Are you sure you want to delete this identity provider? Users who signed up via this SSO will not be able to sign in with it anymore. If password sign-in is disabled in your workspace settings, these users may be locked out completely.
identity-provider.no-claims: No claims returned by the identity provider.

mysql.template: Below is an example to create user {user}{'@'}% and grant the user with the needed privileges.

postgresql.template: Below is an example to create user '{user}' and grant the user with the needed privileges.

console.snowflake: The external console URL managing this instance (e.g. AWS RDS console, your in-house DB instance console)

sentence.create-admin-user: This is the connection user used by Bytebase to perform DDL and DML (non-SELECT) operations.
sentence.create-admin-user-non-sql: This is the connection user used by Bytebase to perform write and admin operations.
sentence.create-readonly-user: This is the connection used by Bytebase to perform read-only operations such as SELECT query.
sentence.create-readonly-user-non-sql: This is the connection used by Bytebase to perform read-only operations.

clickhouse.template: Below is an example to create user '{user}' with password {password} and grant the user with the needed privileges. First you need to enable ClickHouse SQL-driven workflow {link} and then run the following query to create the user.

mysql.template: Below is an example to create user {user}{'@'}% with password {password} and grant the user with the needed privileges.

postgresql.template: Below is an example to create user '{user}' with password {password} and grant the user with the needed privileges. If the connecting instance is self-hosted, then you can grant SUPERUSER.
postgresql.warn: If the connecting instance is managed by the cloud provider, then SUPERUSER is not available and you should create the user via that provider's admin console. The created user will have provider specific semi-SUPERUSER privileges. You should grant Bytebase privileges with 'GRANT role_name TO bytebase;' for all existing roles, otherwise Bytebase may not access existing databases or tables.

redis.template: Below is an example to create user {user} with password {password} and grant the user with the needed privileges.

snowflake.template: Below is an example to create user '{user}' with password {password} for {warehouse} and grant the user with the needed privileges.

sentence.firewall-info: If you use inbound firewall rules for your database instance, please refer to Bytebase Cloud connection instruction.

google-cloud-sql.instance-name-tips: The instance connection name should like {instance}.

mysql.template: Create a service account named {user}, then add it in your Google Cloud SQL as IAM user {user}{'@'}'%'. Grant this user with the needed privileges.

postgresql.template: Create a service account named {user}, then add it in your Google Cloud SQL as IAM user {user}{'@'}project-id.iam. Grant the user with the needed privileges.

transaction-mode.execution-order-note: Non-transactional statements will be executed last.
transaction-mode.label: Transaction Mode
transaction-mode.off-tooltip: Executes statements individually in auto-commit mode. Statements are committed immediately and cannot be rolled back if a later statement fails.
transaction-mode.on-tooltip: Wraps all statements in a single transaction (BEGIN/COMMIT).

issue.unfinished-resolved-issue-tips: This issue is marked as "Done" but some of its tasks are not executed successfully.

use-auth-app.description: Open the two-factor authenticator (TOTP) app on your mobile device to view your authentication code.

role-selection.technical-note: Adds "SET ROLE {role};" to your SQL statement.

state.reopen-confirm: Are you sure to reopen this plan?

plan.subtitle: Draft database changes and run pre-checks before creating an issue for approval.

conversation.select-or-create: Select or {create} a conversation to start.

ai.text-to-sql-placeholder: Use natural language and Bytebase will convert it into SQL

environment-tier.description: The environment will appear differently from other environments.
environment-tier.name: Environment Tier

rollout.auto-info: If all check pass, the change will be rolled out and executed automatically.
rollout.info: Tasks can be executed by users with '{permission}' permission or the following roles.
rollout.name: Rollout Policy
rollout.tip: The policy is applied to issues retroactively.

archive.description: This will archive the selected projects. You can restore them later if needed.
archive.success: Successfully archived {count} project | Successfully archived {count} projects
archive.title: Archive {count} Project? | Archive {count} Projects?

delete.description: This will permanently delete the selected projects and move all databases to the default project.
delete.success: Successfully deleted {count} project | Successfully deleted {count} projects
delete.title: Delete {count} Project? | Delete {count} Projects?
delete.warning: This action cannot be undone. All project data including issues, configurations, and history will be permanently removed.

masking-exemption.expires-in-days: Expires in {days} day | Expires in {days} days

members.allow-ddl: Allow running DDL/DML statements in the selected environments.
members.description: Project members determine the permission to operate database and issues inside the project. Project Owner, Workspace Admin and Workspace DBA all have full project permissions.
members.roles-are-filtered-by-permissions: Roles are filtered by the permissions

overview.info-slot-content: Bytebase periodically syncs the instance schema. Newly synced databases are first placed here. User should transfer them to the proper application project.

settings.confirm-archive-project: Archiving "{name}" will make it read-only and hide it from active project lists. All issues, databases, and settings will be preserved. You can restore it later from the archived projects list.
settings.confirm-delete-project: Deleting "{name}" will permanently remove the project and all associated data including issues, configurations, and history. All databases in this project will be moved to the default project. This action cannot be undone.

allow-jit.description: Once enabled, users can request and use the just-in-time access in the SQL Editor.

allow-request-role.description: Non-project-owner users can request project-level roles themselves

allow-self-approval.description: Allow the issue creator to approve the issue.

ci-sampling-size.description: The maximum number of databases to sample during CI data validation. When disabled (zero), full validation will be performed.

enforce-issue-title.description: Require titles for issues and plans to be created by user instead of auto-generated.

enforce-sql-review.description: Require SQL review checks to pass without errors before allowing issue creation.

labels.description: Labels can be attached in the issues for management

force-issue-labels.description: Require at least one label when creating issues.
force-issue-labels.warning: Require at least configured one label

max-retries.description: The maximum retries count for lock timeout when running the task. The default value is 0. Only applicable to Postgres.
max-retries.self: Maximum retries count for lock timeout

parallel_tasks_per_rollout.description: Control the number of parallel tasks per rollout. Setting this to zero disables any throttling.

postgres-database-tenant-mode.description: The tenant mode for Postgres database. If enable, the issue will be run by the database OWNER. Otherwise, the issue will be run by the instance connection user.

require-issue-approval.description: Issue must be approved before creating rollout.
require-issue-approval.self: Require issue approval before creating rollout

require-plan-check-no-error.description: All plan checks must pass without errors before creating rollout.
require-plan-check-no-error.self: Require plan checks to pass before creating rollout

settings.update-failed: Failed to update the project setting.

issue-approved.label: Notify when all approval steps are completed
issue-approved.title: Issue approved

issue-creation.label: When new issue has been created
issue-creation.title: Issue creation

issue-sent-back.label: When issue is sent back by an approver
issue-sent-back.title: Issue sent back

webhook.activity-support-direct-message: Support send direct messages to related users

creation.desc: Create the corresponding webhook for the {destination} channel receiving the message.

webhook.direct-messages-description: Once enabled, Bytebase will send following events as direct messages to the related users (matched by email) {events}
webhook.direct-messages-tip: Bytebase will fall back to posting webhook messages if no users are matched or the delivery of the message fails.
webhook.direct-messages-warning: You need to configure {im} to make it work.

quick-action.unassigned-db-hint: Newly synced databases start unassigned and must be moved to a project for use.

quick-start.guide: click on the step to try it out

notice.desc: You can still bring it back later from the top right menu
notice.title: Quickstart guide dismissed

refresh-remind.description: Detect outdated version. Please refresh the page.
refresh-remind.title: Outdated version detected

release.change-tip: This change is configured to use a release. Database changes will be executed using the SQL statements from the selected release files.
release.title: Release
release.usage-description: A release is a versioned application artifact whose deployment to an environment is triggered by a GitOps workflow.

release.new-version-content: The new version {tag} is available now! Please check our docs for installation.

role-expire.checkbox: Do not remind until any updates
role-expire.content: Your following roles are going to expire
role-expire.title: Role expiration remind

resource.delete-warning: The "{name}" will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
resource.delete-warning-retry: Please remove it from these resources then retry again.
resource.delete-warning-with-resources: The "{name}" is used by following resources:

resource-id.description: The {resource} ID is a unique identifier. You cannot change it after created.

validation.duplicated: The {resource} ID already exists. Please choose another one.
validation.minlength: The {resource} ID is too short. It should have at least 1 character.
validation.overflow: The {resource} ID is too long. It should have less than 64 characters.
validation.pattern: The {resource} ID can have lowercase letters, digits, or hyphens. It must start with a lowercase letter and end with a letter or number.

gitops-service-agent.description: Permissions for automated CI/CD workflows to create and execute database changes via GitOps.

project-developer.description: All viewer permissions, plus permissions for requesting database changes.

project-releaser.description: All viewer permissions, plus permission for reviewing database change requests for release purposes.

project-viewer.description: Read-only permissions for viewing basic project and database information; users may self-initiate tickets to request data queries and exports.

setting.description: Defining custom roles. You may apply them to project members and used in custom approval.

sql-editor-read-user.description: Read-only permissions for querying database data, without DDL or DML access.

workspace-admin.description: Workspace Admin has all permissions within the workspace.

workspace-dba.description: Workspace DBA has all permissions within the workspace except for managing workspace members.

workspace-member.description: Workspace Member is the most basic role within the workspace.

pending-tasks-preview.description: The following tasks have not been created yet.
pending-tasks-preview.title: Pending Tasks

stage.confirm-create: Confirm to create tasks for this stage?

task.statement-truncated-hint: Statement truncated due to large size.

default.placeholder: Enter default value or leave empty for no default

foreign-key.name-description: The unique name of foreign key

access-token-duration.description: How long access tokens remain valid before requiring refresh. Shorter durations improve security.

api-key.placeholder: An API Key usually starts with sk-*****

ai-assistant.description: Use AI to assist the database development tasks.
ai-assistant.enabled-in-saas: Gemini AI is enabled automatically in the SaaS mode.

endpoint.description: Supply private deployment of API Endpoint.

model.description: Supply private deployment of model name.

allow-admin-data-source.description: When enabled, users can choose between admin and read-only data sources. When disabled, users are forced to use read-only data sources if configured, otherwise fall back to admin data sources.
allow-admin-data-source.self: Allow querying the admin data source

announcement-text.description: To hide the announcement, leave it empty.

audit-log-stdout.description: Output structured JSON audit logs to stdout for ingestion by log aggregators (Splunk, ELK, Datadog, Loki). Requires TEAM or ENTERPRISE license.

default-landing-page.default-view-changed-to-sql-editor: The workspace's default view has been changed to SQL Editor.

sql-editor.description: Use SQL Editor to execute database changes directly, suitable for single-person use or environments that do not require supervision.

workspace.description: Use issue to request, review, rollout, and version database changes, suitable for teams with access control and CI & CD workflow.

disallow-password-signin.description: Users cannot sign in with email & password, only SSO is allowed.
disallow-password-signin.require-sso-setup: A functional SSO must be created first, or users won't be able to log in.

disallow-signup.description: Users cannot self-service signup and can only be invited by the workspace admin.

domain-restriction.description: Specify the domain that could be used as the email domain for workspace members.

members-restriction.description: Restrict sign-in to users with email addresses from the specified domains.

enable-debug.enable: Enable debug level log for the backend service

external-url.cannot-edit-flag: External URL cannot be changed because it is set via --external-url command-line flag
external-url.description: The external URL where user visits Bytebase, must start with http:// or https://.

inactive-session-timeout.description: The session expiration time if no activity is detected for the user. The users will be forced to log out when they reach the limit.

workspace.logo-aspect: The suggested logo size should be 5:2 aspect ratio, e.g 100 x 40.

maximum-role-expiration.description: Maximum role expiration is the maximum period a role can be granted to a user, will apply on all project roles expect the project owner.

rows.description: Limit the output rows in SQL query result.

size.description: Limit the output SQL query result size.

password-restriction.min-length: Minimum length for password should no less than {min} characters
password-restriction.password-rotation: Require users reset their password every {day} days
password-restriction.require-letter: The password must contain at least one letter, regardless of upper case or lower case
password-restriction.require-number: The password must contain at least one number
password-restriction.require-reset-password-for-first-login: Require users reset their password after the 1st time login
password-restriction.require-special-character: The password must contain at least one special character
password-restriction.require-uppercase-letter: The password must contain at least one upper case letter

product-improvement.description: Help improve Bytebase by sharing anonymous usage data

timeout.description: The maximum time allowed for a query to run.

refresh-token-duration.description: How often users must fully reauthenticate. Configuration updates require users to sign in again for changes to take effect.

require-2fa.description: Users who do not have two-factor authentication enabled for their personal accounts will be forced to configure.
require-2fa.enable: Require two-factor authentication for all users

workspace.title: Workspace title

watermark.description: Display watermarks on pages, including username, ID and email.

im-integration.description: The IM integration will be used to push direct messages to users for specific issue events. Works with the project webhook.

auth.description: OAuth authentication is required on first connection. Ensure you have appropriate Bytebase permissions.
auth.title: Authentication Required

mcp.description: Connect AI assistants to Bytebase using the Model Context Protocol.

first-prompt.description: Once connected, try asking your AI assistant:
first-prompt.example: List all projects in my Bytebase instance
first-prompt.title: Your First Prompt

quick-start.description: Alternatively, use one of these CLI commands.
quick-start.title: Option 2: CLI Commands

setup.add-to-config: Add this JSON to your MCP client configuration file.

mcp.title: MCP Integration

entra-sync.description: Sync users and groups from Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) or Okta to your Bytebase instance.
entra-sync.endpoint-tip: Users and groups will be synced to Bytebase via this endpoint.
entra-sync.reset-token-warning: You need to update the application provisioning config.
entra-sync.secret-token-tip: The verification token for the endpoint. Please keep it in secret.

form.email-tips: The email should be unique, and it cannot be changed after creation.
form.title: Title

groups.workspace-domain-required: Please set a workspace domain first.

members.reset-service-key-alert: This action cannot be undone. The old key will stop working immediately. Are you sure you want to reset this service key?
members.revoke-access-alert: Are you sure to revoke access?
members.revoke-allusers-alert: Revoke "All users" will revoke related roles from all other users in your workspace. Are you sure to continue?
members.select-account-hint: You can search for users and groups, or input the entire email to select the service account or workload identity.
members.service-key-copied: Service Key copied to clipboard, please keep it in secret.
members.workload-identity-project-hint: Leave empty to allow all projects
members.workload-identity-repo-hint: Leave empty for all repositories in the organization
members.workload-identity-tag-hint: Leave empty to allow all tags

profile.phone-tips: Be sure to include the country code +1xxxxxx

error.slice-invalid-number: Slice start or end is not a valid number
error.substitution-length: Substitution should be less than 16 bytes

full-mask.substitution-label: Substitution is the string used to replace the original value, the max length of the string is 16 bytes.

inner-outer-mask.inner-label: Inner mask masks the interior part of a string. The prefix and suffix represent the unmasked parts of the data.
inner-outer-mask.outer-label: Outer mask masks the left and right ends of a string. The prefix and suffix represent the masked parts of the data.

md5-mask.salt-label: Salt is used to generate a hash from the original value.

range-mask.label: Range mask will replace the original value from start index to end index (end not included) with the substitution. The index starts from 0.

classification.guide-classification: "classification" maps each category by ID. Leaf categories must reference a level number; parent categories omit the level.
classification.guide-hierarchy: IDs use a dash-separated hierarchy (e.g., "1" is a parent, "1-1" and "1-2" are its children).
classification.guide-intro: Edit the classification JSON below. An example template is provided when no classification is configured.
classification.guide-levels: "levels" defines sensitivity tiers with consecutive numbers starting from 1 (e.g., 1 = Public, 2 = Internal, 3 = Confidential).

global-rules.description: Use global rule to apply masking in batch, e.g. all columns named as "email" will be masked with the specific semantic type and apply the masking algorithm.

sensitive-data.remove-classification-tips: Remove the classification for this column?
sensitive-data.remove-semantic-type-tips: Remove the semantic type for this column?

semantic-types.label: Columns having the same semantic type will use the same masking algorithms.

template.description: You can apply built-in semantic types from the template. Built-in template cannot be changed.

sso.description: Single Sign-On (SSO) is an authentication method that enables users to authenticate with multiple applications and websites via a single set of credentials.

form.auth-url-description: The link to OAuth login page
form.base-dn-description: The base DN to search for users
form.bind-dn-description: The DN of the user to bind as for search requests
form.configuration-description: Configure the connection settings for your identity provider
form.domain-optional-hint: Optional: Use to concat with identifier if it is not in email format
form.general-setting-description: Provide basic information about your identity provider.
form.groups-description: Used for group-based access control (OIDC providers only)
form.identifier-tips: It should be a unique string. And if it is not in email format, it will be spliced with domain.
form.identity-provider-needed-information: Create your SSO application with the following information.
form.in-header-description: Send credentials using HTTP Basic Authorization
form.issuer-url-description: The OIDC issuer URL (well-known configuration will be auto-discovered)
form.ldap-description: Connect with LDAP providers like Microsoft Active Directory, OpenLDAP
form.microsoft-entra-template-description: Sign in with Microsoft Entra ID
form.name: Name
form.name-description: The display name to be shown to the users
form.oauth2-description: Connect with OAuth 2.0 providers like Google, GitHub, GitLab
form.oidc-description: Connect with OpenID Connect providers like Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), Okta, Auth0
form.openid-scopes-description: Space-separated list of OIDC scopes (openid is required)
form.provider-type-readonly-hint: Provider type cannot be changed after creation
form.redirect-url-description: It will be used to fill in the appropriate field in the identity provider application form.
form.scopes-description: A space-separated list of scopes to be carried when accessing the Auth URL
form.skip-tls-warning: Only enable this for development or testing purposes
form.template-description: Choose a pre-configured template or set up a custom OAuth 2.0 provider
form.token-url-description: The API address for obtaining accessToken
form.type-description: Choose the type of identity provider you want to configure
form.user-filter-description: The filter to search for users (%s will be replaced with username)
form.user-info-url-description: The API address for obtaining user information by accessToken
form.user-information-mapping-description: Maps the field names from user info API to the Bytebase user.

purposes.self: What's your main purpose with Bytebase?

workflow.simple: No complex workflow, just need UI tools
workflow.team: Work with teammates, need CI/CD pipeline

hint-tips.duplicate-folder-name-content: Do you want to merge all subfolders and files into the existing folder "{folder}"?
hint-tips.non-empty-folder-content: Do you want to move all worksheets into the root folder or just delete them?

sql-editor.access-grants-redirect-hint: Go to {link} to view and manage your own access grants.
sql-editor.activate-confirm: Are you sure you want to activate this access grant?
sql-editor.allow-admin-mode-only: Instance {instance} is accessible in admin mode only.

batch-export.tooltip: Select at most {max} databases to batch export latest query results

batch-query.description: Batch query {database} additional databases and {group} database groups under {project}.

select-data-source.not-match: Data source mismatch, expect {expect} but got {actual}.
select-data-source.tooltip: Will find available data sources with restriction policy, then apply the selection.

sql-editor.can-not-execute-query: Cannot execute query when loading data.
sql-editor.choose-folder-tips: You can create a new folder by entering a non-existent path. The folders should be joined with a "/".
sql-editor.connection-lost: Connection lost. Will try to reconnect when executing next query.
sql-editor.enable-ddl-for-environment: You can enable the DDL/DML statement for the environment {environment}.

confirm-to-close-unsaved-sheet.content: Close the sheet and lose any unsaved data?
confirm-to-close-unsaved-sheet.title: Close the unsaved sheet?

no-project.contact-the-admin-to-grant-access: Contact to the admin to grant access.
no-project.not-member-of-any-projects: You are not any member of any projects.

sql-editor.notify-empty-statement: Please input your SQL statements in the editor
sql-editor.only-select-allowed: Only read-only statements are allowed to execute.
sql-editor.private-desc: Only you can access this sheet
sql-editor.project-read-desc: Both sheet owner and users with "bb.worksheets.manage" permission can read/write, other project users with "bb.worksheets.get" permission can read
sql-editor.project-write-desc: Both sheet owner and project users with "bb.worksheets.get" permission can read/write

query-context.admin-data-source-is-disallowed-to-query: Admin data source is disallowed to query.

sql-editor.revoke-confirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this access grant?
sql-editor.rows-upper-limit: reached the limit of query results
sql-editor.search-scope-column-description: Column with {type} type. Enter a value and press Enter to jump to the matching row.
sql-editor.search-scope-row-number-description: Enter a row number to jump to that row.
sql-editor.table-empty-placeholder: Click Run to execute the query.
sql-editor.unmask-warning: User will access unmasked data through this query
sql-editor.url-copied-to-clipboard: The URL is copied to your clipboard

errors.description: Auto Completion might be limited or disabled.
errors.title: WebSocket connection failed

attach-resource.label: You can apply the SQL review policy to environments or projects. The database will use the project-level policy if it exists, or fallback to the environment-level policy.
attach-resource.label-environment: The SQL review policy will affect all databases belonging to the selected environment.
attach-resource.label-project: The SQL review policy will affect all databases belonging to the selected project. This will supersede the database environment-level policy.
attach-resource.override-warning: Following resources already bound with other SQL review policies. Click "{button}" will override the config.

basic-info.display-name-label: A display name to help identifying among different review policies.
basic-info.name: Basic info

sql-review.description: SQL review policy can define different set of SQL lint rules for the respective environments. It helps teams to adopt SQL best practice and enforce schema consistency across different databases. Whenever you attempt a DDL/DML change or use SQL Editor to query data, the query will be checked against the configured SQL review policy.
sql-review.input-then-press-enter: Input the value then press enter to add
sql-review.no-permission: Only DBA or Admin has permission to create or update review policy.
sql-review.policy-create-failed: Failed to create the review policy.
sql-review.policy-update-failed: Failed to update the review policy.
sql-review.title: SQL Review

subscription.contact-to-upgrade: Contact your Workspace admin to upgrade the plan.
subscription.description: You can upload your Bytebase license to unlock pro/enterprise features.

instance-assignment.missing-license-attention: You need to assign license to the instance to enable this feature.

overuse-modal.description: You are using features unavailable in the {plan}. Upgrade now to ensure continued access:

subscription.overuse-warning: {neededPlan} on {currentPlan} will be restricted. Upgrade now to ensure continued access.

purchase.cancel-confirm: Are you sure you want to cancel your subscription?
purchase.cancel-pending: Your subscription has been canceled and will end on {date}.
purchase.enterprise-description: Scale your business securely while staying compliant.
purchase.free-description: Designed for solo devs, side-projects, and experiments.
purchase.pending-description: Please wait while we confirm your payment. This usually takes a few seconds.
purchase.pro-description: Built for teams creating production-ready applications.

subscription.request-n-days-trial: Request {days} days trial (no credit card required)
subscription.require-subscription: This feature requires {requiredPlan} subscription, please purchase Bytebase license to unlock it.
subscription.trial-for-days: You can start a free trial for {days} days - no credit card required.

failure.description: Please check if your license is valid.
failure.title: Failed to update license

success.description: Premium features in subscription plan unlocked.
success.title: Successfully updated license

instance-count.runoutof: You have run out of your {total} instance quota.
instance-count.title: Instance count limit

user-count.remaining: Your total user quota is {total}, with only {count} users remaining.
user-count.runoutof: You have run out of your {total} user quota.
user-count.title: User count limit
user-count.upgrade: Upgrade to get more user quota.

sql-review.description: Analyze the SQL statement for potential issues and provide recommendations. Includes built-in rules and your custom rules.

task.data-export-creator-only: Only the issue creator can execute data export tasks

error.scheduled-time-must-be-in-the-future: Scheduled time must be in the future

task.no-permission: No permission to perform this action

online-migration.advice-split-statement-to-single-online-: Advice splitting this statement to single online migration issue.

task.prior-backup-tips: Backup affected data ahead of time. Only applies to data changes (UPDATE, DELETE), not schema changes.

run-immediately.description: Tasks will start executing as soon as you click Run. No delay or scheduling required.

schedule-for-later.description: Set a specific date and time for tasks to automatically start running. Useful for maintenance windows or off-peak hours.

task.skip-prior-backup-description: Skip the automatic data backup before applying changes

log-viewer.multiple-replicas-notice: Server restart detected during execution. Logs are grouped by replica.

rollback.available: Rollback available for {n} task | Rollback available for {n} tasks

preview-statement.description: Bytebase generates a rollback statement based on the task run. You can review the statement before executing the rollback.

status.waiting-max-tasks-per-rollout: Waiting for other tasks to finish. Maximum running tasks limit per rollout has been reached. Last report at {time}.

two-factor.description: Two-factor authentication provides an extra layer of security for member accounts. When signing in, you will be required to enter the security code generated by your Authenticator App.

disable.description: You are about to disable two-factor authentication for your account. You will no longer be required to enter the security code generated by your Authenticator App when signing in.

messages.2fa-required: Two-factor authentication is required in your workspace. Please enable it before you can continue.
messages.cannot-disable: You cannot disable two-factor authentication because your admin requires it in the workspace.

recovery-codes.description: Recovery codes can be used to access your account in the event you cannot receive two-factor authentication codes.

keep-safe.description: These codes are the last resort for accessing your account in case you lose your two-factor authentication code. If you cannot find these codes, you will lose access to your account.
keep-safe.self: Keep your recovery codes in a safe spot.

download-recovery-codes.tips: Keep your recovery codes as safe as your password. We recommend saving them with a password manager such as Lastpass, 1Password, or Keeper.

setup-steps.recovery-codes-saved: I have saved my recovery codes

setup-auth-app.description: Use a phone app like Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, or Authy to get 2FA codes when prompted during sign-in.
setup-auth-app.expired-notice: This setup session has expired. Please regenerate to continue.

scan-qr-code.description: Use an authenticator app from your phone to scan. If you are unable to scan, {action} instead.

setup-auth-app.verify-code: Verify the code from the app

your-two-factor-secret.copy-succeed: Secret copied to clipboard. Paste it into your app.
your-two-factor-secret.description: Copy the code and enter it in your TOTP app manually.
